name: Venous Thromboembolism - Anticoagulants Without VTE Diagnosis (Tricky)
description: **TRICKY test case.** Tests ability to find patients on therapeutic anticoagulants who do NOT have a formal VTE diagnosis on their problem list. These represent the cross-indication problem: patients on warfarin/DOACs for atrial fibrillation, mechanical valves, or post-op DVT prophylaxis. An LLM searching only Condition resources will miss these — and an LLM searching only MedicationRequest will find too many. The provider needs to know that anticoagulant use alone does not equal VTE.
prompt: Find patients prescribed anticoagulants (warfarin, apixaban, rivaroxaban, dabigatran, or enoxaparin) who do NOT have a formal venous thromboembolism diagnosis. These are patients on long-term anticoagulation for other indications.

prompts.naive: Find patients on blood thinners without a clot diagnosis.
prompts.broad: Find patients prescribed therapeutic anticoagulants (warfarin, DOACs like apixaban or rivaroxaban, or LMWH like enoxaparin) who do NOT have a formal VTE/DVT/PE diagnosis on their problem list — typically indicating use for AFib, mechanical valves, or post-op prophylaxis.
prompts.expert: I need MedicationRequest resources for any anticoagulant (warfarin 11289, apixaban 1364430, rivaroxaban 1114195, dabigatran 1037042, enoxaparin 67108) WHERE the patient does NOT have a Condition with VTE codes (128053003, 234044007, 281595001, 59282003, 234049002). FHIR servers don't natively support 'NOT EXISTS' subqueries, so this is best done as two queries (anticoagulant patients minus VTE-diagnosed patients) client-side.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Path C: anticoagulant patients without VTE Condition. Tests cross-indication awareness.
